meaning: n.弹性,适应性,机动性,挠性
examples: A certain amount of flexibility is built into the system.
Computers offer a much greater degree of flexibility in the way work is organized.
The flexibility of distance learning would be particularly suited to busy managers.
The ASSP approach is becoming less attractive due to the flexibility requirement stated above.
But they adjusted, and now give the program high marks for flexibility and choice.
